I found search results for the OnRobot Screwdriver and screw-bit system. Here's what I found:

## Overview

The OnRobot Screwdriver is an intelligent tool for light industrial and collaborative robots that provides intelligent error detection and multiple screw size handling to ensure consistent results and reduce cycle time.

## Screw-Bit System Assembly

The first step is to know what type of screw will be used, as the screw type will define what type of screw fix (if any), screw carrier, bit, and bit holder shall be used. To assemble, place the bit into the bit holder.

## Bit Holders

The system includes two types of bit holders: Bit Holder A generates a higher magnetic force and is commonly used for larger screws, while Bit Holder B generates a lower magnetic force and is commonly used for smaller screws. Bit extenders are also available as a long version of the bit holders.

## Installation on Screwdriver

Simply mount the Screwdriver on any leading robot arm and install the appropriate bit, then enter screw length and torque directly into the robot's user interface.

## Screw Specifications

The screwdriver handles screw sizes from M1.6 to M6 up to 50mm long.

For more detailed assembly instructions, you may want to consult the official OnRobot Screwdriver datasheet, which appears to contain comprehensive assembly guidance.

Based on the search results, here's how the OnRobot screwdriver bit holder retract mechanism works:

## How It Works

Screws are retracted inside the tool and driven automatically once the robot arm moves into position, which reduces robot arm movement and simplified programming. 

The system features a built-in active Z-axis that provides the movement during screwing/unscrewing so the robot doesn't have to move.

## Retraction and Safety

The system has a built-in automatic safety feature to retract and hide the screw bit system (screws up to 35 mm can be fully hidden). More specifically, screws up to 35mm long are retracted completely inside the Screwdriver when moving until the screwdriving process is safely initiated, enhancing its collaborative capabilities.

To improve safety, it also retracts and hides the screw after pickup so the screw and bit cannot hit the operator.

## Safety Features

Safety stop is automatically activated if a screw is exposed to excessive force along its axis, with alert lighting to indicate the issue.

While the search results describe the functionality and benefits of the retraction system, they don't provide specific technical details about the spring-loaded mechanism itself. The mechanism appears to be an integrated internal system that automatically retracts the bit holder using the embedded Z-axis when the screwdriver is not actively screwing.

There are three potential outcomes when testing a self-tapping screw:

1. The screw goes all the way in and is tightened with the set target torque. This is

successful operation.

2. The screw breaks while screwing in and the Screwdriver returns a result code / runtime

error: 10 - “Torque dropped unexpectedly”. This means that the screw cannot handle such
high torque on a material that hard.

3. The Screwdriver stops halfway through and returns a result code / runtime error: 4 -

“Torque exceeded prematurely”. This means that a higher torque is needed to go through
that material with that screw. A solution could be to set a higher tightening torque.

For a successful tapping, ensure that the hole is made according to the screw manufacturer
specifications.

Screw-bit System

This system will highly increase the efficacy of the screws to be picked up, aligned with the
bit, moved around with the Screwdriver and screwed in/out. Therefore, it is highly
recommended to set up the Screw-bit System correctly to keep a high success rate.

set it up correctly.

Screws

The first step is to know what type of screw is going to be used. The screw type will define
what type of screw fix (in any), screw carrier, bit, and bit holder shall be used.

NOTE:

Use a chamfer for better reliability when making the screw hole.

The recommended screw types for the Screwdriver are the ones that have the properties
mentioned previously in the Supported Screws tables.

The bit holder generates a magnetic force that will keep the screw attached and aligned to
the bit.

There are two types of bit holders:

12

 DATASHEET

• Bit Holder A: Generates a higher magnetic force. It is commonly used for the bigger and

heavier screws.

• Bit Holder B: Generates a lower magnetic force. It is commonly used for the smaller and

lighter screws.

WARNING:

If Bit Holder A is used for smaller and lighter screws instead of Bit Holder B, the
screws can jump from the Screw Feeder to the Screwdriver because of the
higher magnetic force.

Bit Extenders 50 and 100 mm

The bit extenders are a long version of the previously described bit holders. Bit extenders are
useful to reach narrow spaces.

The bit extenders have a lock nut to tighten against the screw carrier to ensure that the screw
carrier does not move out of position over time.

When the bit extenders are mounted on the Screwdriver, the maximum total radial runout can
be up to 0.5 mm (measured below the thread as shown in the following picture).

The bit extenders need to be purchased separately by contacting your vendor where the
Screwdriver was purchased.

공식 데이터시트에서 핵심 정보를 모두 찾았습니다. 결과를 정리하겠습니다.

---

## OnRobot Screwdriver - Bit Holder (Screw-bit System) 장착 방법

### 핵심 발견: 공식 데이터시트(v1.6)의 정확한 절차

공식 매뉴얼에 따르면, Bit Holder는 **자석으로만** 샹크에 고정되는 구조입니다. 하지만 장착 전에 반드시 **샹크 위치 조정**과 **Quick Changer 분리**가 선행되어야 합니다.

---

### Screw-bit System 조립 순서 (샹크 장착 전)

1. **비트를 비트 홀더에 삽입** - 비트(1/4" HEX, 25mm)를 비트 홀더 안에 넣음
2. **스크류 캐리어를 비트 홀더 위에 장착** - 스크류 헤드가 캐리어 위에 안정적으로 앉도록 위치 조정 (헤드와 캐리어 사이에 갭이 없어야 함)
3. **스크류 캐리어 높이를 정밀 조정** - 나사 헤드가 캐리어에 밀착되도록 조절
4. **스크류 픽스 삽입** - (DIN 912, ISO 4762, ISO 14579, ISO 14580, ASME B18.3 HEX Cylinder 타입만 해당) 스크류를 빼고 스크류 픽스를 밀어넣음

---

### Screw-bit System을 스크루드라이버에 장착하는 절차 (문제 해결 핵심)

**Step 1.** 로봇 UI 또는 Web Client에서 **샹크를 최대한 위로(highest possible value)** 올림

**Step 2.** **Quick Changer에서 스크루드라이버를 분리**

**Step 3.** **뚜껑(lid)을 제거**

**Step 4.** 비트 홀더의 **hex 형상** 부분을 스크루드라이버 **샹크 끝부분 안쪽에** 삽입. 자석의 힘으로 고정됨

**Step 5.** 비트 홀더가 완벽히 장착되었는지 **가볍게 흔들어서** 확인. 흔들리면 안 됨

---

### 문제 상황 분석 및 해결책

설명하신 증상(샹크가 뒤로 밀림, 자석으로 고정 안 됨, 손으로 당기면 빠짐)에 대한 원인과 해결책입니다.

**원인 1: 샹크가 최상단 위치가 아님**
- Quick Changer에서 분리하면 전원이 끊겨서 샹크 위치를 제어할 수 없음
- **해결**: 분리하기 **전에** 먼저 UI에서 샹크를 최대값(highest)으로 올려놓아야 함. 그래야 샹크가 위로 올라간 상태에서 비트 홀더를 삽입할 공간이 확보됨

**원인 2: Hex 형상 정렬 불량**
- 비트 홀더 끝은 hex(육각) 형상이고, 샹크 내부도 hex 형상임
- 정렬이 안 맞으면 자석이 완전히 밀착되지 않아 헐거워짐
- **해결**: 비트 홀더를 약간 회전시키면서 hex 형상이 맞물리도록 삽입

**원인 3: 비트 홀더 타입 확인**
- **Bit Holder A**: 자력이 강함 (큰 나사용)
- **Bit Holder B**: 자력이 약함 (작은 나사용)
- 작은 나사에 A를 쓰면 스크류 피더에서 나사가 뛰어오를 수 있고, 큰 나사에 B를 쓰면 고정력 부족

---

### 분리 방법 (참고)

1. UI에서 샹크를 **최대한 밖으로(highest possible value)** 이동
2. 제공된 **키(key) 도구**로 비트 홀더를 잡음
3. 키를 잡은 상태에서 UI로 **샹크를 안쪽으로(lower value)** 이동시키면 비트 홀더가 분리됨

---

### 요약

| 단계 | 작업 | 주의사항 |
|------|------|---------|
| 1 | UI에서 샹크 최상단으로 이동 | **Quick Changer 분리 전에** 수행 |
| 2 | Quick Changer에서 분리 | |
| 3 | 상단 뚜껑(lid) 제거 | |
| 4 | 비트 홀더 hex를 샹크 안에 삽입 | 육각 정렬 맞추어 밀어넣기 |
| 5 | 흔들어서 고정 확인 | 흔들리면 재삽입 |

**가장 중요한 포인트**: 샹크를 UI에서 최상단으로 올린 후에 Quick Changer를 분리해야 합니다. 샹크가 아래에 있는 상태에서 분리하면 스프링/모터 힘이 없어서 샹크가 뒤로 밀리고, 비트 홀더를 제대로 삽입할 수 없습니다.
